moduleName: Time Value of Money in Finance
name: Present Value (single cash flow)
description: Calculates the present value of a single future cash flow discounted at rate r for n periods.
example: PV of $1,000 received in 3 years at 5%: PV = 1000/(1.05)^3 = $863.84

moduleName: Time Value of Money in Finance
name: Future Value (single cash flow)
description: Calculates the future value of a present cash flow compounded at rate r for n periods.
example: FV of $1,000 invested for 5 years at 6%: FV = 1000 × (1.06)^5 = $1,338.23

moduleName: Time Value of Money in Finance
name: Present Value of Annuity
description: Present value of a series of equal payments made at equal intervals.
example: PV of $500 annual payment for 10 years at 8%: PV = 500 × (1 - 1.08^-10)/0.08 = $3,355.04

name: Holding Period Return
description: Total return earned from holding an asset over a single period, including price appreciation and income.
example: Buy at $100, sell at $110, dividend $3: HPR = (110-100+3)/100 = 13%

name: Geometric Mean Return
description: Compound average rate of return over multiple periods. Always ≤ arithmetic mean.

name: Continuously Compounded Return
description: Return calculated using natural logarithm. Additive across time periods.

name: Population Mean
description: Average of all values in a population.

name: Sample Variance
description: Measure of dispersion using deviations from sample mean, with Bessel's correction (n-1).

name: Bayes' Formula
description: Updates the probability of event A given new evidence B. Foundational for conditional probability.

name: Two-Asset Portfolio Variance
description: Variance of a portfolio of two assets, incorporating their weights, individual variances, and correlation.

name: Roy's Safety-First Ratio
description: Measures how many standard deviations the expected return is above the minimum acceptable return. Higher is better.

moduleName: Portfolio Risk and Return: Part II
name: Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM)
description: Expected return of an asset based on its systematic risk (beta) relative to the market.

moduleName: Portfolio Risk and Return: Part II
name: Sharpe Ratio
description: Risk-adjusted return: excess return per unit of total risk. Higher is better.

moduleName: Fixed-Income Bond Valuation: Prices and Yields
name: Macaulay Duration
description: Weighted average time to receive all cash flows, weighted by present value. Measured in years.
example: 3-year 5% annual bond at par: MacDur ≈ 2.86 years
variables: PV(CFt) = present value of cash flow at time t

moduleName: Fixed-Income Bond Valuation: Prices and Yields
name: Modified Duration
description: Approximate percentage change in bond price for a 1% change in yield. Key measure of interest rate risk.

moduleName: Fixed-Income Bond Valuation: Prices and Yields
name: Dollar Value of a Basis Point (DV01)
description: Change in bond price for a 1 basis point (0.01%) change in yield.
variables: DV01 = dollar value of a basis point

moduleName: Fixed-Income Bond Valuation: Prices and Yields
name: Price Change with Duration and Convexity
description: Estimated percentage change in bond price using both duration (first-order) and convexity (second-order) effects.

moduleName: Equity Valuation: Concepts and Basic Tools
name: Gordon Growth Model (Constant Growth DDM)
description: Intrinsic value of a stock assuming dividends grow at a constant rate forever.

moduleName: Equity Valuation: Concepts and Basic Tools
name: Justified P/E Ratio (Leading)
description: Fundamental P/E ratio derived from the Gordon Growth Model, where b is the retention ratio.

name: Put-Call Parity
description: Fundamental relationship between European call, put, underlying asset, and bond prices. Must hold to prevent arbitrage.
variables: PV(X) = present value of strike price

name: Current Ratio
description: Liquidity measure: ability to pay short-term obligations. Higher = more liquid. >1 generally considered healthy.
variables: Current Assets = assets convertible to cash within 1 year
Current Liabilities = obligations due within 1 year

name: DuPont Analysis (3-factor)
description: Decomposes ROE into profit margin × asset turnover × financial leverage. Reveals drivers of equity returns.

name: Weighted Average Cost of Capital
description: Blended cost of all capital sources weighted by their proportion in the capital structure. Used as discount rate for firm valuation.
variables: WACC = weighted average cost of capital
